description: Database schema for accessibility training system

training_sessions.description: Stores unique training session codes for intermediate training. Each session has a unique code that users can use to resume their training.

unique_code.description: Unique 9-character code (format: XXXX-XXXX) used to identify and resume training sessions

created_at.description: Timestamp when the training session was created

answers.description: Stores user answers for intermediate training questions. Each row represents one answer to one question within a training session.

training_session_id.description: References the training session this answer belongs to

question_number.description: The question number (1-20 for intermediate training)

question_type.description: Type of question: multipleChoice (single answer), trueFalse (boolean), or multipleSelect (multiple answers)

user_answer.description: User's answer stored as comma-separated indices (e.g., '0,2' for multiple select, '1' for single choice). Indices correspond to the options array in intermediateQuestions.json

answer_status.description: Status of the answer: Correct, Incorrect, or Not answered

created_at.description: Timestamp when the answer was first created

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the answer was last updated

sent_codes.description: Tracks when training session codes are emailed to users for record-keeping purposes

email.description: Email address where the code was sent

code.description: The training session code that was sent

sent_at.description: Timestamp when the code was emailed

questions.description: Basic training questions (10 questions). Used for basic training quiz. Stored in session, not database.

intermediateQuestions.description: Intermediate training questions (20 questions). Answers stored in database. Questions referenced by id (1-20).

get_session_by_code.description: Get training session by unique code

get_answers_for_session.description: Get all answers for a training session
get_answers_for_session.sql: SELECT question_number, answer_status, user_answer FROM accessibility_manual.answers WHERE training_session_id = $1 ORDER BY question_number

get_answer_status_map.description: Get answer statuses as a map for a session
get_answer_status_map.sql: SELECT question_number, answer_status FROM accessibility_manual.answers WHERE training_session_id = $1

get_sessions_with_stats.description: Get training sessions with answer statistics
get_sessions_with_stats.sql: SELECT ts.*, COUNT(a.question_number) as total_answers, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.answer_status = 'Correct' THEN 1 END) as correct_answers FROM accessibility_manual.training_sessions ts LEFT JOIN accessibility_manual.answers a ON ts.id = a.training_session_id GROUP BY ts.id

available_metrics: Question-level performance (which questions are answered correctly most/least)
Completion rates (sessions with all 20 questions answered)
Average time to complete (using created_at timestamps)
Codes sent via email (from sent_codes table)

key_relationships.training_sessions -> answers: One session can have many answers (up to 20 for intermediate training)
